div.head: The Secretary of State to the Ambassador in Chile (Culbertson)The same, mutatis mutandis, on the same date, to the Ambassador in Peru as telegram No. 22.
div.p: 26. I desire to know at the earliest possible time the final points on which Chile and Peru disagree as to the settlement. From all the cables, it seems to us that only the question of where the harbor will be located remains to be settled. President Hoover is anxious for an immediate settlement and he will do anything he can to further it. I desire the above information so as to advise him just what steps to take in communicating with the two Governments.
